result:
name things for what they do, not how they do it — `fetchUser` not `handleUserStuff`

one function, one job — if you write "and" in name, split it

keep related code close — file by feature, not by type (auth/ not controllers/ + models/ + utils/ scattered)

flatten early returns, kill nesting — guard clauses at top, happy path at bottom

pull magic numbers and strings into named constants at top of file

stop passing 6 args — bundle into object, or sign function is doing too much

delete dead code instantly, don't comment it out — git remembers

when you get lost, draw arrows on paper: who calls who, what flows where — the confusing arrow is where abstraction is wrong

rule of thumb: if you can't explain what function does in one sentence, it's doing too much
